description: Golden bill corpus manifest for mechanism-backed quantification and control-path evaluation.

bills.title: American Rescue Plan Act of 2021
bills.inclusion_rationale: Contains explicit federal appropriation and transfer amounts suitable for direct fiscal extraction.
minimum_required_sources: official cost estimate or budgetary scoring
exclusion_risks: downstream implementation effects may be mixed with direct transfers

bills.title: Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023
bills.inclusion_rationale: Appropriations sections provide explicit top-line fiscal quantities for direct-fiscal mode.
minimum_required_sources: conference report or enacted summary tables
exclusion_risks: omnibus structure can fragment numeric clauses across divisions

bills.title: Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act
bills.inclusion_rationale: Imposes recurring reporting duties that map to labor-hour and vendor-cost compliance burden estimation.
exclusion_risks: rulemaking updates can alter compliance frequency assumptions

bills.title: Short-Lived Climate Pollutants: Methane Emissions: Dairy and Livestock: Organic Waste: Landfills
bills.inclusion_rationale: Creates measurable compliance activities for local programs and regulated entities with recurring operational burdens.
exclusion_risks: local program heterogeneity can increase uncertainty ranges

bills.title: Fast Food Restaurant Franchisor Responsibility Act
bills.inclusion_rationale: Labor cost changes and franchise cost shocks are suitable for pass-through incidence assumptions.
exclusion_risks: simultaneous local policy changes can confound pass-through attribution

bills.title: Fair Wage Act of 2016
bills.inclusion_rationale: Minimum-wage increases provide a canonical pass-through setup for business-to-consumer incidence analysis.
exclusion_risks: regional heterogeneity can widen confidence intervals

bills.title: Inflation Reduction Act of 2022
bills.inclusion_rationale: Consumer-facing credits and incentives support explicit take-up assumptions under bounded adoption scenarios.
exclusion_risks: program awareness and administrative friction can shift realized take-up

bills.title: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act
bills.inclusion_rationale: Program funding pathways with eligibility constraints enable take-up modeling across infrastructure subsidy channels.
exclusion_risks: multi-program structure can blur single-channel adoption attribution

bills.title: Ceremonial California Assembly Concurrent Resolution (Control)
bills.inclusion_rationale: Resolution-format content should fail closed because no stable cost mechanism exists.
exclusion_risks: title keywords can trigger false fiscal matches in weak retrievers

bills.title: House Resolution with Non-Fiscal Recognition Language (Control)
bills.inclusion_rationale: Non-binding recognition text should deterministically route to non-quantified fail-closed output.
exclusion_risks: symbolic references to funding can be misread as appropriations

bills.title: Worker Status: Employees and Independent Contractors (AB 5)
bills.inclusion_rationale: Used as one side of a numeric ID collision pair to test resolver discipline and false mode carry-over.
exclusion_risks: policy salience can bias retrieval toward commentary over statute text

bills.title: Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R. 5)
bills.inclusion_rationale: Pairs with California AB 5 to ensure the corpus detects and isolates same-number different-jurisdiction collisions.
exclusion_risks: search tools may collapse AB/H.R. identifiers when query is underspecified
